title: Workflow
description: A published workflow definition. Routing is embedded in each node — there is no separate edges array.

config.description: Trigger-specific configuration (cron expression for scheduled, webhook secret, form ID, etc.)

raw_json.description: Original JSON payload from the builder, kept for audit / re-import.

routing_start_task_action.description: Single successor used by: start, task, action.

next.description: ID of the next node to execute after this one completes.

routing_condition.description: Condition routing: evaluates an expression and picks yes or no branch.

condition.description: Boolean expression evaluated against instance data. Example: 'amount > 5000'.

next_yes.description: Node ID to follow when the condition is true.

next_no.description: Node ID to follow when the condition is false.

routing_parallel.description: Parallel routing: fans out to multiple branches simultaneously.

next_branches.description: List of node IDs to execute concurrently. All branches must complete before execution continues past a downstream merge node.

routing_merge.description: Merge routing: waits for all required-input branches before continuing.

required_inputs.description: Node IDs whose branches MUST arrive before the merge proceeds. Typically these are the last nodes of each required parallel branch.

optional_inputs.description: Node IDs whose branches are accepted if they arrive early but are not waited on.

task_fields.description: Human task configuration — only relevant when type == 'task'.

assigned_role.description: Role group that receives this task (e.g. 'manager', 'finance_team', 'hr').

assigned_position.description: Specific position within the role (e.g. 'CFO', 'Department Head').

assigned_user.description: Direct user fallback. Used only when assigned_role is empty.

task_actions.description: Actions the assignee is allowed to take.

next_actions.description: Maps each task action to the next node ID. When the assignee picks an action (e.g. 'approve'), the workflow follows the corresponding branch. Only used when a task has multiple possible outcomes.

form_template_id.description: ID of a form template the assignee must fill in before the task can be completed.

sla_days.description: Deadline in business days. 0 means no SLA enforced.

connector_field.description: External-service connector — only relevant when type == 'action'.

params.description: Key-value parameters forwarded to the connector. Values may use {{data.fieldname}} template syntax to reference instance data.

params.subject: Expense Request: {{data.title}}
params.body_template: Please review the expense of {{data.amount}} submitted by {{data.submitted_by}}.
